verdicts.note: MD5 vs SHA1 hasher are structural clones. FIPS fix (usedforsecurity=False) applies equally to the sha1 hashlib call in the lagging member.

trans_null stubs get_language_from_request vs get_language both just return LANGUAGE_CODE but have different signatures/roles. The i18n-pattern fix concerns request handling, irrelevant to the parameterless get_language stub. Trivial coincidental match.
'Dropped support for Python 3.8 and 3.9' is a maintenance/version change, not a behavioral bug fix.

RightTests vs LeftTests are clones, and the Right() zero-length fix is a real bugfix, but Left() did not have the zero-length defect; the divergence is intentional and LeftTests needs no change. Also these are test classes, not the buggy code.
create vs get_or_create on reverse FK manager are clones; clearing prefetch cache after writing applies equally to get_or_create which also creates instances.
'Added deduplicate_items parameter to BTreeIndex' is a new feature, not a bug fix.
RemoveIndex vs RemoveConstraint are clones, but 'display meaningful symbols for each operation' is a UX/feature enhancement to makemigrations output, not a behavioral bug fix.
hashed_file_path and cached_file_path test helpers both do fullpath.replace(STATIC_URL, ''); the protocol-relative/STATIC_URL='/' fix would plausibly need to update both identically.
'Dropped support for PostgreSQL 13 and PostGIS 3.0' is a maintenance/version-drop change, not a bug fix.

find() (with all= param) and as_sql() are unrelated abstract stub methods that only coincidentally both raise NotImplementedError with a docstring. The 'all' name-shadowing fix has nothing to do with as_sql.

Members are sibling test model fixtures: GeneratedModelNullVirtual (db_persist=False, virtual generated column) vs GeneratedModelNull (db_persist=True, stored). They are deliberately distinct variants that test both code paths. The bugfix changes full_clean() behavior, not these model definitions; the divergence (virtual vs stored) is intentional, so the fix would not need to propagate to the sibling.
Subject 'Created AlterConstraint operation' is a new feature, not a bug fix. RemoveConstraint and RemoveIndex are genuine analogous clone classes, but no behavioral bug was fixed.

Subject 'Added CSP support' is a feature addition, not a bug fix. The two members are trivial one-line stubs (get returns HttpResponse, xview returns HttpResponse) in different test files; trivial-stub match, not meaningful clones.

acheck_password (async) and check_password (sync) are the async/sync clone pair. The change 'Optimized acheck_password by using sync_to_async' is an async-specific optimization, not a behavioral bug; the sync/async divergence is intentional and the change is not applicable to the sync sibling.

Subject changes the DEFAULT_AUTO_FIELD setting default (config change), not a behavioral bug fix. Members are test Migration fixtures with intentionally different dependencies; they explicitly hardcode AutoField on purpose for these tests.

Same settings-default change (not a bug fix). The two Migration members differ substantially (lagging has extra AddField/AlterUniqueTogether operations and initial=True); they are distinct test fixtures, not real clones.

Settings-default change, not a behavioral bug fix. Members create entirely different models (SillyModel vs Book) in unrelated test migration fixtures; only the boilerplate CreateModel/AutoField scaffolding matches.

Bugfix (lazy type-annotation evaluation in tag registration) is real but touches Library code, not these test classes. SimpleTagRegistrationTests vs InclusionTagRegistrationTests are analogous test classes that intentionally exercise different tag types; the touched test member is not a logic site the fix must propagate from.

Subject 'Fix readme' is a docs fix, not a behavioral bug fix. Members (record_rpc_latency vs service) are unrelated abstract methods that merely both end in 'raise NotImplementedError()'; not real clones.

Subject 'Fix python typos' is a non-behavioral typo/docstring fix. unary_stream_event and stream_unary_event are genuine mirror clones, but no behavioral bug was fixed; the argument-positioning divergence is intentional and correct.

indirectInterface is byte-identical in both files, but the lagging copy lives in tpl/internal/go_templates, a vendored/frozen copy of Go's text/template kept in sync with upstream rather than hand-patched. The Context-first-arg fix is real, but propagating into the vendored Go template internals is not how Hugo maintains that tree, so divergence may be intentional.
Plainify (StripHTML) and Base64Encode (base64 encode) share only the cast.ToStringE boilerplate; their core logic is unrelated. Not a genuine clone pair.

Both newDeps are test setup helpers and already differ (one sets contentDir and ContentSpec/Log differently). 'Fix raw TOML dates in where/eq' is a real fix but the lagging test helper is its own fixture with legitimately different config; no reason it must mirror the other helper's edit.
New (returns *Namespace{deps}) and newTocExtension (returns &tocExtension{options}) are unrelated trivial constructors that coincidentally share a one-line struct-literal return. Not the same logic.

addDefaultHeaders is byte-identical in resources/.../remote.go and tpl/data/data.go. 'Fix order when reading custom headers in resources.GetRemote' is a real behavioral fix that would apply equally to the identical sibling.
TableOfContents and Content on LazyContentProvider are Type-3 clones (init.Do(ctx) then delegate). The cross-site uninitialized-output-format fix plausibly affects both lazy delegators.
pageContentOutput.TableOfContents and .Content are Type-3 clones (initInit then return). Same uninitialized-output-format fix plausibly applies to both.

imageCache.clear and createCountingFs.Reset share the lock/reset-map shape (Type-4) but operate on different domains. The 'Processed images count regression for multiple languages' fix is specific to the image cache; the unrelated file-count fs reset would not need the same change.
TestAppendNilsToSliceWithNils and TestSortStable share only the integration-test scaffold (loop 4x, build, assert). They exercise different features; not the same logic.

Two getParam implementations are near-identical type-switches over Params(); the fixed copy adds explicit time.Time/map cases instead of a bare default. 'Fix GroupByParamDate with raw TOML dates' is a real fix that the lagging copy (still using default: return v) plausibly also needs.

fixed=TestConfigParamSetOnLanguageLevel (language params) vs lagging=TestConfigOutputFormatDefinedInTheme (output formats) are unrelated config tests, not the same logic. Bugfix subject 'relative import issue in libsass/dart sass' is unrelated to either test and only incidentally touched the file.

Stat and Open are thin wrappers both calling fs.doStat(name); they share the error-handling preamble but intentionally diverge (return fis[0] vs newUnionFile). The overlapping-mount regression almost certainly lives in doStat, not these wrappers, so the fix would not need to be duplicated into Open.

Leaf-bundle vs branch-bundle rebuild tests share the edit-and-assert pattern (Type-3) and the subject is a genuine fix. But leaf vs branch are deliberately different scenarios with different assertions/render counts; the fix to the leaf test's expectations does not obviously apply to the branch test.

IdentifierBase and BaseNoLeadingSlash have identical bodies (return p.Base()[1:]) — true Type-1/2 clone. Subject 'Fix rebuild with resources.Concat' is a real fix touching path identifiers. Bodies are already identical here so propagation is trivially satisfied; flagged as a genuine divergent-edit hazard candidate.

Two MarkStale methods (contentNodeIs vs resourceSources) with identical nil-guarded loop structure over a slice — genuine Type-3 clone. Subject is a real rebuild bug fix and the stale-marking logic plausibly needs to be kept in sync across both collection types.

fixed=TestSitemapShouldNotUseListXML vs lagging=TestMenuHasMenuCurrentSection are entirely different tests (sitemap vs menu, different domains). n_members=30 indicates an over-broad cluster of unrelated integration tests. The sitemap fix is irrelevant to the menu test.

ParseBytes vs ParseBytesMain are Type-2 clones differing only by lex entry point (lexIntroSection vs lexMainSection) — that difference is intentional. The shortcode-at-start fix almost certainly lives in the lexer, and the two entry points exist precisely to handle different starting sections, so the wrappers should not converge.

NewResourceTypesProvider and NewProvider are both factory functions returning a holder struct but belong to unrelated domains (resource types vs markup converter) with different fields/types. Shared 'constructor returning struct literal' shape is too generic to be a real clone. The NameNormalized panic fix concerns resource types only.

Cache.Drain and SliceCache.Reset share the Lock / reassign-map / Unlock concurrency pattern (Type-3 clone). Subject 'Fix concurrent map read and map write' is a genuine concurrency fix, and both cache variants are the concurrency-sensitive siblings that would need the same locking discipline.

Stat and Open share the doStat+error-check boilerplate (genuine Type-3), and 'Fix same resource file published more than once' is a real bug fix. But the shared snippet shown is unchanged; Stat returns fis[0] while Open unions via newUnionFile, so the dedup fix likely lives in doStat/newUnionFile, not in this body. Unclear the same change applies to Open. Uncertain.

'all: Run modernize -fix ./...' is an automated modernization refactor, not a behavioral bug fix ('fix' is incidental). Also the two benchmark functions (XXHash vs DecodeCSS) are unrelated, not clones.
Same automated 'modernize -fix' refactor, not a real bug fix. BenchmarkGetOrCompileRegexp and BenchmarkCSSEscaper are unrelated benchmarks, not clones.

'Fix term title when taxonomy name contains spaces' is a genuine bug fix, but the fixed_member is a NEW test (TestTaxonomiesSpaceInName) added for it, and the lagging_member (TestTaxonomiesDirectoryOverlaps) is an unrelated pre-existing test. They are not clones and nothing needed to propagate.

'all: Run go fix ./...' is an automated tooling refactor (e.g. reflect.Ptr->Pointer rename), not a behavioral bug fix. Also IsValid and isNil are deliberately inverted-logic functions, an intentional divergence, not a clone needing the same change.

Short/Double deserializers are genuine parallel clones, but 'Fix lossy conversions flagged by Java 20' is a compiler narrowing-warning cleanup (behavior-neutral), not a real behavioral bug. The narrowing is specific to the small-type (short) accumulation; DoubleDeserializer uses a long accumulator, so the change does not apply to it anyway.

Both are trivial delegating constructors calling this(Utils.propsToMap(properties), ...). The fix ('committed API in PrototypeAsyncConsumer timeout') concerns consumer timeout behavior, not this constructor, and the snippet is unchanged. The two constructors belong to unrelated classes (consumer vs producer) and share only boilerplate; no meaningful clone logic to propagate.

ApplicationEvent and BackgroundEvent are near-identical abstract base classes (genuine clones), and the fix (release assignment on close) is real. But the change adds COMMIT_ON_CLOSE/LEAVE_ON_CLOSE to ApplicationEvent.Type enum; BackgroundEvent legitimately has its own distinct event types. The shared structure (id/type/equals/hashCode) is unchanged. Divergence is intentional; fix does not apply to the sibling.

Subject is explicitly 'fix flaky test' — excluded per criteria. Additionally AbstractCoordinator constructor and the deprecated MemberDescription constructor are unrelated delegating constructors, not clones.

DescribeClusterOptions and DescribeTopicsOptions are genuine option-class clones, but 'Fix double negative typos in Javadoc and exception messages' is a docs/typo fix ('cannot not supply' -> 'cannot supply'), not a behavioral bug fix.

test_dtype_multi_index (dtype=) and test_converter_multi_index (converters=) are Type-3 clones, but the fix targets str->IntegerDtype conversions; converters use a different code path so propagation is doubtful.

Genuine clone pair (Float vs Int test_equals). Fix is NaN handling specific to FloatingArray.equals; integer arrays don't carry float NaN semantics, so the integer sibling likely doesn't need the same NaN change.
check_opname/skip methods vs _check_divmod_op are unrelated methods. Also 'TST: fix Nullable xfails' is test maintenance, not a behavioral bug fix.
test_names_option_wrong_type vs test_utf16_encoding are unrelated tests; neither relates to the to_xml/pd.NA fix. Detector mis-grouped two pytest.raises tests.
to_array_error (Floating) vs to_boolean_array_error (Boolean) share structure, but the fix concerns Integer/Floating mismatched NAs; Boolean is out of scope, so divergence is intentional.

Both members are trivial pytest fixtures returning request.param (val vs full_indexer) with different purposes; matching on boilerplate, not real shared logic. Fix is to setitem behavior, not these fixtures.
BUG/REF: replacing NUMBA_FUNC_CACHE with lru_cache is a refactor, not a behavioral fix. The two numba tests check different error conditions ('numba does not support' vs 'The first 2').
test_center_reindex_frame vs test_center_reindex_series are genuine clones (frame/series variants). rolling apply ValueError fix plausibly affects both axes/structures.
Interval vs BaseOffset .pyi stubs are different classes. TYP fix to mid/length is a type-annotation change, not a behavioral bug fix.

test_concat_bad_type and test_concat_bad_columns share a pattern but assert different error conditions. The fix adds a NEW nlevels check; each sibling intentionally tests a distinct validation, so the column test wouldn't need the type-check change.

Fix SAS7BDAT RLE formula is a real bug fix, but fixed_member test_0x40_control_byte and lagging test_many_columns are distinct tests over different SAS/CSV files and encodings. They only share a read_sas->assert_frame_equal pattern; the lagging test is pre-existing and unrelated, not a copy needing the same change.

TYP: fix ReadPickleBuffer is a type-annotation fix, not a behavioral bug. Members BaseBuffer (IO buffer protocol stub) and IntervalMixin (interval stub) are entirely unrelated Protocol classes. Both checks fail.

groupby.nth-as-filter is a real bug fix and the two tests are near-identical Type-3 clones, but they intentionally differ: nth at index 1 yields nulls_fixture while first/last 'last' yields 3. The nth semantics fix does not apply to first/last, which legitimately diverge.
STYLE: fix pylint reimported warnings is a lint/style change, not a behavioral bug fix. Members (CBMonthEnd vs CBMonthBegin test classes) are real clones but the commit is non-behavioral.

Fix NameError in tab complete warning test is a real test bug fix. The two test_tab_complete_warning methods are genuine clones (identical logic, different object name/file). The fixed member gained @async_mark(); the lagging copy plausibly needs the same fix.

mean/median-with-strings is a real bug, but fixed_member asserts grouped.agg raises TypeError while lagging test_apply_convert_objects asserts an identity apply equality. The detector matched only on the large shared DataFrame literal; the actual test logic differs entirely.

Arrow CSV test fix is real and the two tests are Type-3 clones, but they intentionally test different input values ('None' vs '0') leading to different pyarrow warning expectations. The divergence is by design; the fix targets the None-value case only.

Real mean/median bug fix; the string vs category parametrized tests are structural clones, but they legitimately differ in expected error messages (string 'Could not convert' vs category 'does not support aggregation'). The message change applies only to the string path.

The two tests (np.sum keepdims vs initial parameter validation) are genuine clones, but the bugfix subject 'mean/median with strings' is unrelated to np.sum parameter validation; the commit only touched this file incidentally. Fix does not apply to either member.

Real mean/median bug, but test_agg_cython_table_raises_series (asserts could-not-convert raise) and test_transform_and_agg_err_series (transform-vs-aggregator ValueError) are different tests with different logic and fixtures, not a clone pair.

The two usecols 'additional columns' tests are genuine Type-3 clones (integer vs string column headers). The bugfix subject is about Arrow CSV parser erroring on dtype for index_col, which only tangentially relates; the commit likely touched many test files. Unclear the index-dtype fix needed propagation into the sibling string-header usecols test. Ambiguous.

Both call infer_dtype_from_scalar but assert fundamentally different dtypes (datetime M8[ns] vs np.bool_). The fix concerns non-nano datetime inference; the boolean test is unrelated and legitimately differs. Not the same logic for propagation purposes.

Near-identical parallel tests (to_timedelta/timedelta64[ns] vs to_datetime/datetime64[ns]). The fix targets to_timedelta with ArrowDtype duration specifically; the to_datetime sibling already worked and need not change. Likely the timedelta test mirrors an existing datetime test. Propagation questionable.

The two 'any' methods (DataFrame override in frame.py vs generic.py) are genuine clones, but the bugfix subject is about merge sort behavior, completely unrelated to 'any'. The commit incidentally touched these files; the fix does not apply to the lagging 'any'.

TYP commit (type-annotation fixes), not a behavioral bug fix. Members are different functions: read_table() public signature vs TextReader.__init__ stub in parsers.pyi, with different parameter sets. Not genuine clones.

Sibling tests for GroupBy.value_counts categorical single grouper (observed=False vs observed=True), genuine Type-3 clones. The sorting-order bug affects expected_index ordering; both tests assert ordering and plausibly both needed updating. Genuine hazard.

Commit is a test adjustment for new string dtype, not a behavioral product bug fix. The two tests share only a large data-fixture literal but test entirely different behavior (groupby agg partial failure vs apply convert objects). Clone match is on the fixture, not the logic.
STY commit fixing doctest/docstring formatting, not a behavioral bug fix. Members boxplot_frame_groupby and hist_series are different plotting functions, not clones.

test_str_fullmatch and test_str_match are near-identical clones, but the fix concerns str.fullmatch wrongly matching partial strings. str.match SHOULD match partials, so the divergence is intentional and the fix must not propagate to the match test.

Genuine Type-3 sibling tests for groupby.transform('sum') with missing labels (matching+missing vs missing-only). The BUG/PERF fix for unobserved categories exercises the same transform-with-missing-labels path; both plausibly affected. Genuine hazard.

test_utf16_encoding vs test_missing_prefix_with_default_namespace are unrelated XML tests sharing only pytest.raises+read_xml scaffolding; different assertions/inputs. Subject is an ENH/CI fix, not a behavioral bugfix.
is_interval_dtype vs is_period_dtype are genuine Type-2/3 clones, but 'DOC: fix SA01' is a docstring-validation lint fix, not a behavioral bug fix.
is_datetime64_dtype vs is_timedelta64_dtype are clones, but 'Fix docs for api.types' is a documentation fix, not behavioral.
Shares a large DataFrame literal but the two tests have entirely different logic (groupby agg raising vs apply identity). Not real clones; subject is a TST/test-only fix.
Parallel test_groupby_raises_*_np functions are clones, but the change is a TST xfail/error-message update in test code, not a production behavioral bugfix.
is_period_dtype vs is_interval_dtype are clones, but 'DOC: fix deprecation message' edits docstring/warning text, not behavior.
boxplot_frame_groupby vs hist_series are different plotting functions with different signatures/bodies. Not clones; subject is a DOC docstring-validation fix.

Genuine BUG fix in production code, and the two tests are sibling clones (list-agg vs single-agg variant). But these are intentionally differing test variants covering distinct agg inputs; the lagging single-agg test does not need the list-agg change.

Real subplot-count bugfix; the two test functions have identical bodies (Type-1). Bodies are already identical so no in-body divergence to propagate; these are sibling parametrized-style tests, fix is in production code. Ambiguous whether a propagation hazard exists.

render_git_describe_long vs render_git_describe are clones but legitimately differ (unconditional vs conditional distance). Subject is 'DOC: ... fix minor typos', a docs/typo fix, not behavioral; divergence is intentional.

Structurally identical Type-2 test clones, but fixed tests tz_localize(None) while lagging tests tz_convert(None) — different methods. The tz_localize Arrow fix does not apply to tz_convert; divergence is intentional.
Both are sibling tests of str.match(case=False) on identical data, structurally near-identical. The str.match Arrow optional-groups fix plausibly affects the lagging case-kwarg test path too.
fixed tests str.fullmatch; lagging tests str.startswith/endswith — different operations, different data shape. Not the same logic; fullmatch fix does not apply.

fixed_member test_map_engine_not_executor (engine validation error) is unrelated to lagging test_map_arg_as_kwarg (arg->func rename warning). The bugfix subject is about map with pyarrow timestamp/duration; the shown fixed_member is only incidentally in the commit. Not clones.

test_match_compiled_regex and test_fullmatch_compiled_regex are near-identical (match vs fullmatch) exercising the same case/pat/flags handling for compiled patterns. The mismatched-flags fix shares the same code path; should propagate.
DOC: Fix ES01 is a docstring/See-Also documentation fix, not a behavioral bug fix.
DOC: Fix ES01 documentation-only change; also fixed (hist_series) and lagging (boxplot_frame_groupby) are different plotting functions.
DOC: Fix ES01 documentation-only; fixed (parallel_coordinates) and lagging (boxplot_frame) are different functions, not clones.

sum and prod are identical _reductions wrappers (Type-1 clones), and the subject is a genuine bug fix, but it concerns sum of empty str-dtype series with min_count. The shown member bodies are unchanged (real fix is elsewhere), and prod of a string series is not a valid operation, so the fix does not apply to prod.
DOC: Fix ES01 adds an explanatory docstring paragraph; documentation-only, not a behavioral bug fix.

bugfix_subject is 'DOC: Fix ES01...' a docstring/documentation fix, not a behavioral bug. Also fixed=boxplot_frame_groupby vs lagging=hist_series are different plotting wrapper functions, not the same logic.
fixed=Timedelta stub vs lagging=Interval stub in different .pyi files: entirely different classes, not a clone pair. Subject is largely a docstring/stub-compat change for NumPy 2.4.0.
fixed=BlockPlacement stub vs lagging=IndexEngine stub: different classes in different .pyi files, not clones. Same numpy-compat stub commit, no shared logic.

Genuine near-identical test clones (union vs intersection) and a real bug fix. But these are test functions, and the fix concerns _union sorting; the intersection mutation-safety test is a legitimately separate sibling rather than a missed propagation.

is_signed vs is_unsigned_integer_dtype are genuine clones and the timedelta64-deprecation fix is real, but timedelta64 is a numpy signedinteger subclass; the unsigned variant is unaffected, so the change does not apply to the lagging member.

Two test variants: the 'unitless' test uses np.datetime64('nat') with no unit while the sibling explicitly passes 'ms'. The unit difference is the deliberate point of the variant; the explicitly-unitted test does not need the change.

Bug fix is about out-of-bounds datetime in replace. fixed=find_result_type_floats vs lagging=find_result_type_uint_int are parametrized clones for different dtype families; the uint/int variant is a separate intentional case, not unrelated to the datetime fix.

Fix adds _str_getitem (str slicing) support. fixed=test_str_slice vs lagging=test_str_start_ends_with test different str operations with different expected dtypes; not the same logic and startswith/endswith is unaffected by the slice fix.

sdsll2str (src/sds.c) and hi_sdsll2str (deps/hiredis/sds.c) are byte-identical vendored clones. The LLONG_MIN negation overflow fix in -value applies equally to the hiredis copy, which was not patched. Genuine divergent-edit hazard.

aeApiFree in kqueue and epoll backends are trivial close+free clones, but the fix is a kqueue-specific read/write event timing issue. epoll does not have that bug, and aeApiFree is not where the timing logic lives; the cloned function was likely touched incidentally.

aeApiResize kqueue vs epoll are genuine Type-2 clones (only the kevent/epoll_event struct differs). The commit 'Fix the timing of read and write events under kqueue' touched ae_kqueue.c, but the buggy logic is event ordering/timing, not this trivial zrealloc resize helper. The resize function is unchanged by the fix and there is no relevant divergence to propagate.

cmd_get just replies SimpleString 'OK'; fork_exitcode replies a LongLong exit code. Same boilerplate UNUSED prologue but unrelated bodies/purpose. Not the same logic. Detector grouped two trivially-similar test stubs.

ustime in redis-benchmark.c had the overflow bug ((long)tv.tv_sec); the fix was to cast to (long long). The lagging quicklist.c copy ALREADY uses ((long long)tv.tv_sec) and is correct, so the fix does not need to propagate -- lagging member already has the fixed form.

addReplyPushLen vs addReplyAttributeLen are Type-2 clones differing only in aggregate marker ('>' vs '|'). The fix (#11875) makes push replies reach clients even with CLIENT REPLY OFF|SKIP; attributes have different RESP3 semantics and should not be forced the same way. Divergence is intentional.

ThreadsManager_runOnThreads is a no-op DO-NOTHING stub returning NULL; rioConnWrite is an unsupported-write stub returning 0. Both are tiny UNUSED-arg stubs but serve unrelated subsystems. The crash-report fix does not concern rioConnWrite. Not genuine clones.

HelloBlock_RedisCommand in test module (blockonbackground.c) vs example module (helloblock.c) are real clones but already intentionally divergent (test uses 3-elem targ with time-tracking flag and BlockDebug_ThreadMain; example uses 2-elem targ and HelloBlock_ThreadMain). The race-condition fix targets the test/module-thread path; the example module is not maintained in lockstep, so applicability is ambiguous.

Same clone pair as 115. The fix adds blockClientPrivdataInit(bc) to the test module copy. The example helloblock.c lacks it, but the two copies already diverge intentionally (time-tracking arg). Whether the example needs the same init is unclear; example modules are often not kept in sync. Ambiguous.

Subject is 'Fix typos in multiple Redis source files' -- a comment/typo fix (e.g. 'caasn'->'can' in a comment), not a behavioral bug fix. zslNthInLexRange vs zslNthInRange are genuine clones but the change is non-behavioral.

ACLListFreeSds and freeHintsCallback are both one-line sdsfree wrappers (weak Type-2 match). Subject 'Fix incorrect parameter type reports' concerns command/parameter type metadata, not these free callbacks; the function bodies are unchanged by such a fix. The 'fix' does not apply to this logic.

defragStageExpiresKvstore vs defragStageDbKeys are sibling defrag stages sharing a common prologue but with intentionally different defragfns and helper callbacks (expires is ref-only; db keys handles key/val via dbKeysScanCallback). The fix is specific to the expires comparison/defrag path; the db-keys stage uses a different mechanism and does not need the same change.

getStringConfigCommand (test stub returning strval, ignores name) vs HelloACL_UserChanged (auth callback resetting global_auth_client_id) are unrelated functions with different signatures and logic. Detector grouped them only because both use REDISMODULE_NOT_USED macros.

setBoolConfigCommand and set_bool_config are genuine clones, but both explicitly REDISMODULE_NOT_USED(name). The fix is about passing unprefixed name to callbacks; these stubs ignore name entirely, so the fix cannot apply. Commit touched the file incidentally.
getBoolConfigCommand vs get_bool_config are genuine clones but both ignore name (REDISMODULE_NOT_USED). The 'pass unprefixed name' fix is irrelevant to functions that discard name.

testTempDir vs tempDir are near-identical but intentionally differ: tempDir adds os.RemoveAll(dir) while testTempDir does not. Two distinct helpers serving different test needs. 'fix temp directory handling in some tests' is test-infra tweaking, not a behavioral product bug, and the divergence is intentional.

TestContext2Apply_providerAlias vs TestContext2Apply_basic are parallel tests using different fixtures (apply-provider-alias vs apply-good) and different expected strings. 'fix test fixtures with multiple providers' modifies test data, not a product bug, and the tests legitimately differ by design.

bugfix_subject is a merge commit ('Merge pull request ... jbardin/test-fix') with no concrete behavioral fix description; it is a test fix. The two destroy-mode tests use different fixtures/IDs and are separate scenarios.

Both are testUnparsedVariableValue.ParseVariableValue mock helpers in different packages. The fixed one was upgraded to a struct carrying value+source; the lagging one is the older string-based form using ValueFromNamedFile. These mocks intentionally differ per package/test; not a divergent-edit hazard.

TestCloud_planWithRequiredVariables vs TestCloud_applyWithRequiredVariables are parallel plan/apply tests that intentionally differ (plan vs apply operation, ValueFromCLIArg vs ValueFromNamedFile, different output header asserted). Designed-in divergence, not a missed fix.

bugfix is about local vars being overridden by remote vars during import. TestRemote_planOtherError vs TestCloud_planOtherError assert different backend-specific error strings ('remote backend...' vs 'Terraform Cloud...') and are intentionally distinct; the import-variable fix does not relate to these planOtherError tests.

Two assertDiagnosticCount helpers; fixed_member checks 'len(diags) != 0' while lagging checks 'len(diags) != want'. The fixed version ignores 'want' and looks like the weaker/diverged copy. The bugfix subject ('module loader nil pointer panic') doesn't clearly relate to this helper diff, suggesting the file was touched incidentally; relationship between the fix and the divergence is ambiguous.

Two distinct test schema-builder fixtures (one adds new_field, the other adds leaf_block_device). They are Type-3 similar boilerplate but represent intentionally different test scenarios; the sensitive-schema fix may not need identical mirroring.

Both bodies are trivial 'return fmt.Errorf("unimplemented")' stubs but on different interface methods. Subject 'Fix test compilation errors caused by interface change' is a mechanical compile fix, not a behavioral bug fix.
Unlocking() (state locker) vs Stopping() (operation) are different one-line print methods. The JSON-output fix targets state locking actions specifically; the operation Stopping method is unrelated.

Two parallel e2e init tests (Type-3 clones). Fix sets TF_CLI_CONFIG_FILE='' to avoid plugin-cache pollution in the local-only test. The vendored test reaches the network and may not need the same isolation; also a test-reliability fix rather than a product bug.
Identical Close() mock methods in current and legacy provider_mock.go. Subject 'fix locking and race in test mocks' is a genuine race fix; the legacy identical copy plausibly needs the same locking.

GetType (type switch over JSON values) vs absMoveableEndpointKind (switch over address types) only share a type-switch+panic skeleton. The large-number truncation fix is unrelated to the lagging function.

Two validate tests are Type-3 clones but intentionally differ (one expects code 0 with warnings, the other code 1 with an error). Subject 'fix json -help display and failing tests' is mixed; the test edit is incidental test maintenance, not a propagatable product bug.

TestTest_InvalidOverrides (override-warning expectations) vs TestTest_NestedSetupModules (resource-cleanup assertions) are largely different tests sharing setup boilerplate. Same vague 'fix ... and failing tests' subject; the changes are test-specific maintenance, not a shared bug.

ParseAbsResourceInstance vs ParseAbsResource are parallel parse functions (genuine Type-3 clones) that already legitimately diverge in their case handling. Unclear the 'partial address representation for deferred actions' fix touches these or needs identical mirroring.

ConfigureProvider on providerClose vs ApplyResourceChange on unknownProvider are different methods returning different diagnostics; they only share the tfdiags error-return skeleton. The provider-close memory-leak fix does not map onto the unknown-provider stub.

ReadDataSource vs PlanResourceChange in unknown.go are near-identical deferral stubs (Type-3 clone). Bugfix 'fix deferred data sources and unknown component applies' is a genuine behavioral fix touching deferral handling. The two parallel stubs plausibly need parallel treatment, but they handle distinct request types so propagation is plausible-but-not-certain.

mustAbsComponentInstance vs mustAbsResourceInstanceObject are trivial test parse helpers, structurally identical clones. The bugfix is a real deferral behavioral fix, but these helpers merely parse different address-type strings; a deferral fix has no bearing on them. The file was touched incidentally.

Subject 'fix Descendants spelling' is a spelling/rename refactor (DescendentForInstance), not a behavioral bug. Also movableObjectDeclRange (returns SourceRange via declaration lookup) and moveableObjectExists (returns bool existence check) are not the same logic.

testModuleInline duplicated across two test files; real bugfix 'Avoid duplicated warnings'. The fixed copy intentionally adds loader.AllowLanguageExperiments(true) which the lagging copy lacks by design (different test context). Whether the duplicated-warnings fix applies to both helpers is ambiguous.

TestGRPCProvider_ImportResourceState (Msgpack) vs ...JSON (Json) are parallel tests of the same RPC decode path. Bugfix 'Fix ImportResourceState RPC response decoding for identity data' is a genuine fix; the JSON variant tests the same decode logic and plausibly needed the same expectation update.
These are stringer-generated _() compile-check functions for two unrelated enums. 'fix typo' is not a behavioral bug fix, and generated code is not meaningfully a clone pair to propagate fixes into.

TestCloud vs TestRemote ServiceDiscoveryAliases intentionally differ: cloud workspace uses tags/project, remote uses prefix. The fix 'crash when showing a cloud plan without a cloud backend' is cloud-specific and does not apply to the remote-backend sibling.

makeStaticObjectAddr (ConfigCheckable, static config addresses with kind/mode) and makeDynamicObjectAddr (Checkable, dynamic instance addresses) have different switch structures and purposes. The ephemeral-resources fix likely touched only the static variant; not a real clone pair.

PlanResourceChangeCalled vs ListResourceCalled are trivial mutex-guarded getters returning different bool fields. The reattached-providers fix is real but these accessors merely return distinct flags; nothing in the fix logic applies to the getter sibling.

loadConfig vs loadSingleModule are similar Meta loader helpers, but the bugfix 'Fix ParseVariableValues for different command flows' concerns variable-value parsing, unrelated to these config/module loading helpers; the file/function was touched incidentally and the lagging sibling loads a single module differently by design.
Subject 'Fix grammar in error message' is a wording/grammar fix, not a behavioral bug. Also fixed=Decode test vs lagging=Encode test exercise different functions with different error strings.

Subject 'Fix ParseVariableValues' is unrelated to either clone member (loadConfigWithTests vs loadSingleModuleWithTests). The two functions also load different types (Config vs Module). Commit touched file incidentally.

Real fmt fix for new .tfquery.hcl file type; QueryFiles test needs AllowExperimentalFeatures. MockDataFiles test legitimately tests a different file type and should not gain experimental flag. Divergence intentional.

Subject is about provider-local in generated resource config; these clone members test import-identity error vs success scenarios. The fix subject does not correspond to a change needed in the lagging identity-module test.
Genuine behavioral fix (stop discarding diagnostics). Members are identical-structure stub methods appending errors; 7 of 8 changed, lagging WriteStateBytes plausibly needs same fix.
'Fix things staticcheck complains about' is a lint cleanup, not a behavioral bug fix. Change is removing commented-out code.
staticcheck lint cleanup, not a behavioral bug fix. Members are identical but no real bug.
staticcheck lint cleanup, not a behavioral bug fix.

Real CPU/infinite-select bug fix, but the changed member here is just a one-line constructor delegation in nonblocking socket; the actual fix was almost certainly elsewhere. TServerSocket (blocking) constructor is a sibling but the frameSize/readBuffer bug is specific to nonblocking server, so the fix likely does not apply to the lagging blocking socket.
'add uuid support' is a feature addition (new TType.UUID constant), not a bug fix. javame member intentionally lacks newer types (no ENUM either), so divergence is also intentional.
Subject 'add uuid support for java and kotlin' is a feature addition, not a bug fix. The two TJSONProtocol classes (java vs javame) are genuine clones, but the commit adds new functionality.
'fix various java codestyle issues' is a formatting/lint/style change, not a behavioral bug fix. TBase64Utils copies are genuine clones but the change is cosmetic.

'Fix Java UUID typeid' touches TType. fixed_member adds ENUM=16 and UUID=17; lagging javame variant lacks even ENUM and is a deliberately stripped-down legacy edition, so propagation to javame is doubtful. Genuinely ambiguous.
'Update eslint & prettier' is a lint/formatting tooling update, not a behavioral bug fix. checkRecursively copies are clones but only reformatted.
'Update eslint & prettier' is lint/formatting, not a bug fix. Also TProtocolException and THeaderProtocolError are distinct constructors with different signatures/fields, not genuine clones.

'Fix Python 3.12 build issues' is a real fix, but ntohll and letohll are intentionally different: big-endian vs little-endian byte assembly with deliberately reversed shift amounts. Not the same logic; divergence is by design.
'Fixed Python flake8 offenses' is a lint/style cleanup, not a behavioral bug fix. The two protocol test methods are similar but the change is whitespace/quote style only.

'fix wrong error messages' concerns compile-fail test fixtures. missing_return_type and missing_semicolon_or_return_type are different small test functions, not the same logic; the fix is about expected error output, not the function bodies.

'fix spelling mistakes' is a typo/comment fix (e.g. 'after after'), not a behavioral bug. Also non_cooperative_task and poor_little_task are different test helpers with different loops and return types, not clones.

'fix *_closed false positives' is a real runtime bug fix. read_until_pending in tcp_stream.rs and uds_stream.rs are identical genuine clones, but they are test helpers and the actual fix lives in runtime code, not in these bodies. The helper itself does not embody the fix, so propagation is not clearly warranted.

Identical helper write_until_pending in TCP vs UDS test files - genuine clone. Bugfix '*_closed false positives' is a real behavioral fix. But this is a trivial test helper; the fix to the *_closed logic lives elsewhere, not in this loop. The helper itself needs no change in either file. Divergence here is not a hazard.
ms() is a trivial Duration::from_millis wrapper, identical across two test files. Real bugfix on interval wake-up, but the fix has nothing to do with this 3-line helper. No propagation needed.

Identical blocking() test in rt_threaded_alt.rs vs rt_threaded.rs. Real concurrency bugfix in the alt runtime (multi_thread_alt). The alt-specific test was modified; the standard rt_threaded test legitimately targets the non-alt scheduler and need not receive the same change. Divergence intentional.

local() queue constructor identical between multi_thread_alt and multi_thread. Real concurrency bugfix, but it targets the alt scheduler. The non-alt multi_thread queue is a separate, stable implementation; the constructor shown is unchanged-looking and the alt fixes are deliberately confined to the alt module. Intentional divergence.

The two test functions are near-identical (owned vs borrowed cancellation token variants). But bugfix_subject is 'fix the _owned_test to use an owned cancellation token' - it is fixing the TEST itself (it incorrectly used &token instead of an owned token), not a product behavioral bug. The non-owned variant correctly uses &token, so it must NOT receive the same change. Not a real product bugfix and divergence intentional.

stripStrings (regex stripping quoted strings from an expression) vs toLegacyClass (regex stripping '-from' suffix). Both are one-line regex .replace helpers but are entirely unrelated logic. Detector wrongly grouped on superficial .replace shape. Not clones.

compileWithSrcset vs compileWithAssetUrls are parallel test helpers (srcset vs asset-url transforms) - structural Type-3 clones. The SSR hoisting bugfix touched product code; this is a test helper and the two helpers intentionally differ (different transforms). No propagation of a meaningful change needed.

withDefaults and defineProps are both compile-time macro stubs that just warnRuntimeUsage and return null. The shared body is the boilerplate stub pattern, not real logic; their signatures differ entirely. The withDefaults+defineProps generics fix is a type-signature change specific to withDefaults. defineProps has no params here. Boilerplate stubs, not genuine logic clones; fix not applicable to lagging member.

defineOptions vs defineExpose are macro stubs sharing only the warnRuntimeUsage boilerplate; signatures and purpose differ. Fix makes defineOptions props type optional - specific to defineOptions' ComponentOptions generic. defineExpose takes an exposed record, unaffected. Boilerplate-only similarity, not a real logic clone; not applicable.

createCacheExpression vs createIfStatement are both AST node factory functions returning an object literal, but build different node types with different fields. Shared shape is the factory boilerplate. The hoisting-to-caching bugfix is conceptually about caching nodes; createIfStatement is unrelated. Not genuine clones.

The two getPositionInCode functions are genuine identical clones (only indentation differs), and the commit 'fix(compile-sfc): handle inline template source map in prod build' is a real bugfix. However, the fixed_member is a test HELPER function in compileTemplate.spec.ts that computes line/column from a string offset. The actual bug fix concerns source-map handling in the compiler-sfc production build code, not this position-computing helper. The helper was in a touched test file but its logic is unrelated to the bug. The lagging member is an equivalent independent test helper in compiler-core's tests; neither needs the source-map fix. Fix does not apply to either clone member, so propagation premise fails.
